data.title: Stitch Fix points to generative AI boost in FQ1
data.description:
Stitch Fix (SFIX) rose in postmarket trading on Thursday after topping sales expectations and setting guidance ahead of expectations.

The retailer reported revenue rose 7.3% year-over-year in FQ1 to $342.1 million. The active client count was down 5.2% year-over-year to 2.307 million. Net revenue per active client rose 5.3% during the quarter to $559.

Gross margin decreased 180 basis points to 43.6% of sales. Adjusted EBITDA of $13.4 million was generated during the quarter.

On the balance sheet, Stitch Fix (SFIX) ended the quarter with cash holdings of $244.2 million and no debt.

CEO update: "As a result of the successful execution of our transformation strategy, we are increasingly becoming the retailer of choice for more of our clients' apparel and accessories needs. We are doing this by leveraging the latest in GenAI technology, the expertise of our human stylists, and our assortment of leading brands to deliver the most client-centric and personalized shopping experience"

Looking ahead, Stitch Fix (SFIX) guided for FQ2 revenue of $335 million to $340 million (midpoint $337.5 million) vs. $312 million consensus. Full-year revenue of $1.32 billion to $1.35 billion (midpoint $1.335 billion) is anticipated, vs. $1.31 billion consensus.

Shares of Stitch Fix (SFIX) were up 4.4% in postmarket trading to $4.87 vs. the 52-week range of $2.60 to $6.99. Short interest on SFIX stands at 12.5% of the total float.

data.title: Boise Cascaden to retire; COO set to succeed him
data.description:
• Boise Cascade (BCC) chief executive officer Nate Jorgensen will retire on March 2, 2026, and remain on the board of directors.
  • Chief operative officer Jeff Strom has been unanimously appointed by the board to become CEO effective March 3, 2026.
  • The company does not plan to fill the chief operating officer role after Strom transitions to CEO.

data.title: Domo Non-GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.06, revenue of $79.4M beats by $0.37M
data.description:
• Domo press release (DOMO): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $79.4M (-0.5% Y/Y) beats by $0.37M.
  • Subscription revenue was $71.9 million
  • Billings were $73.2 million
  • Subscription Remaining Performance Obligations (RPO) was $405.9 million as of October 31, 2025, an increase of 15% year over year
  • Current subscription RPO was $214.1 million as of October 31, 2025, an increase of 3% year over year
  • Net cash provided by operating activities was $3.4 million, an increase of 125% year over year
  • Adjusted free cash flow was $2.1 million, an increase of 115% year over year
  • GAAP operating margin was negative 9%, an increase of 5 percentage points year over year
  • Non-GAAP operating margin was positive 7%, an increase of 4 percentage points year over year

data.title: Capricor Therapeutics launches proposed public offering of common stock
data.description:
• Capricor Therapeutics (CAPR) on Thursday said it has begun a proposed underwritten public offering of its common stock and plans to grant underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 15% additional shares.
  • The size and timing of the offering will depend on market conditions.
  • Capricor expects to use the proceeds for development and manufacturing of its product candidates, as well as working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • CAPR -5.90% after hours to $23.92.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tech Voices: Meta's metaverse cuts, Senators vs. China chip sales
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector. 

  • Meta (META) could slash funding for its metaverse division by as much as 30% for the coming year.

The cuts would likely result in layoffs, which could occur as early as January. The division includes the company's Meta Horizon Worlds and Quest VR units, according to Bloomberg, which cited people close to the matter. 

Bloomberg's sources said savings from the cuts are expected to be diverted to Meta's Reality Labs division, which includes AI wearables.

  • A bipartisan group of senators has filed a bill that would prevent the Trump administration from easing restrictions on the sale of AI chips to China in the near term. 

The SAFE Chips Act, which was filed by Republican Sen. Pete Ricketts and Democrat Sen. Chris Coons, would direct the Commerce Department to deny export license applications for advanced U.S. AI chips to China, Russia, Iran, or North Korea for at least 30 months. 

"Denying Beijing access to these AI chips is essential to our national security.  Codifying President Trump’s current AI chip limitations on Communist China secures this goal.  It will allow U.S. chip companies to continue to rapidly innovate and widen our compute lead exponentially," Ricketts said in a statement released Thursday. 

“As China races to close our lead in AI, we cannot give them the technological keys to our future through advanced semiconductor chips.  This bipartisan bill will protect America’s advantage in computing power so that the world’s most next-generation AI models are built at home by American companies, and the world’s infrastructure is built on the American tech stack," Coons said in the same statement.

The filing of the bill comes as the Trump administration considers allowing the sale of Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 AI chips to China.

data.title: GE Aerospace declares $0.36 dividend
data.description:

data.title: Evolution Petroleum reinstated with Buy rating at Roth on 'acquire and exploit' strategy
data.description:
Evolution Petroleum (EPM) +1.3% in Thursday's trading as Roth Capital resumed coverage with a Buy rating and $5 price target, saying the company's diversified production mix, focus on acquiring mature assets with lower declines, and healthy return of capital provide "an interesting platform for continued growth," and believing its recent 12.2% annualized dividend yield is sustainable and covered by free cash flow.

Roth analyst Nick Pope praised Evolution's (EPM) active "acquire and exploit strategy" for M&A, focused on smaller, mature, non-operated assets with lower declines, as the company looks to remain nimble and opportunistic with assets where it feels larger firms have less interest, and believes it will continue to uncover compelling opportunities to augment its current production base; Evolution made acquisitions totaling $44 million in 2024 and $26M YTD in 2025.

Pope said Evolution's (EPM) debt is near the upper end of optimal levels, but the analyst sees it as manageable with low-decline, mature assets, and with the recent restatement of the commitments on its borrowing base, the analyst expects the company will be able to manage additional small acquisitions in the next several years.
